description: Sarah (tm_3) is clearly carrying an outsized load this week: 3 authored PRs, 4 substantive reviews, led the incident response, and was on-call. The pulse should flag her under 'People stretched thin' with multiple citations. The 1:1 prep (with tm_3 as target) should show wins and questions without fabricating feelings.

repo:acme/search.content:
PR #1203: Switch query planner to cost-based reordering

Replaces the hand-tuned reorder heuristics with a simple cost model derived from table statistics. Benchmarks show 18% p95 latency improvement on the top 20 query shapes.

PR #1208: Add a slow-query log sink

Writes every query over 500ms to a dedicated sink so we can triage without sifting the main logs. Pulled out of the planner PR to keep the diff small.

PR #1215: Hotfix: revert cost-based reorder for prefix-match queries

Production showed a regression on prefix-match queries we didn't see in the benchmark suite. Reverts that one code path while we investigate.
Review on #1199 (Marcus's PR, requested changes): The locking scheme here races with the sweeper. I drew it out and I think we need a second mutex on the shard index. Happy to pair on this tomorrow.
Review on #1201 (Diego's PR, commented): Left 12 comments mostly on error handling — the current code swallows the context-cancelled case in 3 places. See inline.
Review on #1205 (Jamie's PR, approved with comments): Approach is right. Two nits on the metric names — please use snake_case to match the rest of the codebase.
Review on #1210 (Priya's PR, approved): Clean. Ship it.

slack:eng-search.content: #eng-search: has anyone seen cost-based planners regress on prefix-match in practice? I'm seeing it in the bench suite for #1203 and I'm not sure if it's my cost model or a query-shape thing.
#eng-search: FYI I'm on-call this week so may be slow on PR reviews in the back half — ping me directly if you're blocked.
#eng-search: hotfix #1215 is rolling out now. This is the prefix-match regression from the planner change. I'll write up what I learned about the cost model next week.

slack:prod-alerts.content: #prod-alerts: taking point on the search latency alert. P95 on /search jumped from 180ms to 620ms starting around 19:45. Investigating.
#prod-alerts (thread): confirmed — it's the prefix-match query shape under the new planner. Prepping a revert PR now.
#prod-alerts: hotfix deployed, p95 back to ~190ms. Will write the postmortem tomorrow.
#prod-alerts (thread): thanks Sarah — I'm around if you need a second pair of eyes.
